_meta.note: Generated artifact - edit the Excel, not this file.

form_questions.question: What's the idea in your own words — what would you love this to help with?
What problem does it solve, and how is that handled today?
Who is the accountable owner for this?
Who will use it, roughly how many, and how often?
Do its outputs materially affect individuals - eligibility, pricing, hiring, complaints, or anything customer-facing?
Could it disadvantage or treat any group or region unfairly?
Walk me through what should happen start to finish - what kicks it off, what comes out?
Should it only suggest or draft things for a person to action, or actually take actions and change things itself?
Does it follow fixed rules, or make judgement calls on messy, varied information?
Is it a single task, or does it plan across several steps and systems and recover if something fails?
Does it mainly create new content, move/process data, or analyse data to predict or recommend?
What information does it use, where does it live today, and how good is it?
Does any of it include personal, customer, or confidential data?
Does it need your organisation's own documents or knowledge to answer correctly?
Which systems does it connect to - and does it only read, or also change/write records?
Will it share data with other departments or outside vendors?
For any decision or action it takes, must a human approve first, or can it act on its own?
How important is it that users can see why or how it produced an answer?
Are there laws, regulations, or company policies it must comply with?
Is there anything it must never do, or any topic or tone to avoid?
What does 'good enough to ship' look like? How fast, how much volume, any deadlines or budget limits?
If it's wrong or unavailable, is that a minor annoyance or a serious business/customer impact?
Level-baseline technical controls switched on automatically once the use case is classified at a given domain + level - not asked of the user.

AU-INF-1.1.original: Are scripts/assistants running only on managed devices with EDR and disk encryption enabled?

AU-INF-1.2.original: Are credentials completely removed from scripts (using SSO/keychain/vault instead)?
AU-INF-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Secrets handling — no creds in scripts.

AU-INF-1.3.original: Will the RDA avoid logging or exposing sensitive data via local files or clipboard?
AU-INF-1.3.rephrase_or_note: Local data-leak prevention. Trigger: Seg 4 sensitivity = yes.

AU-DGP-1.1.original: Will any PII/sensitive data touch local temp files, clipboard, screenshots, or caches?
AU-DGP-1.1.rephrase_or_note: Local handling of sensitive data. Trigger: Seg 4 sensitivity = yes.

AU-DGP-1.2.original: Can we avoid pulling/storing sensitive data locally (e.g. masked fields, no local logs)?

AU-AIG-1.1.original: Who is the accountable human for every RDA action/output on the desktop?

AU-AIG-1.2.original: Will the user see clear on-screen disclosure that an assistant/script is acting (and when)?
AU-AIG-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Should users be clearly told on-screen when the automation is acting for them?

AU-AIG-1.3.original: Will users validate any automation-assisted output before it's used downstream?
AU-AIG-1.3.rephrase_or_note: Should a person check the output before it's used anywhere else?

AU-INF-2.1.original: Are bot service accounts scoped to minimum required entitlements for each system?

AU-INF-2.2.original: Are all secrets stored in a central vault with rotation, not embedded in configs or orchestrator DBs?

AU-INF-2.3.original: Are all bot-system connections protected with TLS 1.2+ (mTLS where supported)?

AU-DGP-2.1.original: What data classes will be processed (PII, special category, financial, customer, employee)?
AU-DGP-2.1.rephrase_or_note: What kinds of information will it handle — personal, financial, customer, employee, or other sensitive data?

AU-DGP-2.2.original: Can we trace input > transformation > output, including source systems and queues?

AU-DGP-2.3.original: Are retention and deletion policies defined for bot artifacts (queues, logs, exports)?

AU-AIG-2.1.original: Will every bot action be immutably logged with timestamp, actor (service account), and payload refs?

AU-AIG-2.2.original: Is there a documented exception taxonomy, plus SLA for human review of failed/flagged cases?
AU-AIG-2.2.rephrase_or_note: When it fails or flags something, who should review it and how quickly?

AU-INF-3.1.original: Are inter-system API calls from orchestrator/workers allow-listed and authenticated (incl. secure webhooks)?

AU-INF-3.2.original: Has HA/DR for orchestrator and workers been designed and tested with explicit RPO/RTO?

AU-INF-3.3.original: Are build/run/approve roles separated with appropriate RBAC and audit logs?

AU-DGP-3.1.original: Will the orchestrator enforce data tagging, retention, and segregation per queue?

AU-DGP-3.2.original: Do any flows export data to other business units or vendors? If yes, are DPAs and purpose limits set?
AU-DGP-3.2.rephrase_or_note: Will it share data with other departments or outside vendors? (If yes, DPA/purpose-limit work tags to Dev/Sec/Legal.)

AU-AIG-3.1.original: Who owns the end-to-end outcome (not just a step)? Is the escalation path documented?
AU-AIG-3.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: owner (Seg 1); escalation path confirmed in Seg 6.

AU-AIG-3.2.original: Is periodic human sampling/QC defined for critical steps and exception queues?
AU-AIG-3.2.rephrase_or_note: Should a person spot-check its work on important steps from time to time?

AU-AIG-3.3.original: Could routing/priority rules cause unfair outcomes (e.g. queue starvation)? If yes, how is it checked?
AU-AIG-3.3.rephrase_or_note: Could the way it prioritises work unfairly disadvantage some cases, customers, or groups?

AU-INF-4.1.original: Are AI/ML endpoints (IDP, classifiers, GenAI) vetted and secured (auth, network, rate-limits)?

AU-INF-4.2.original: How are third-party models/frameworks (images/tokenisers) scanned and pinned in the supply chain?

AU-INF-4.3.original: Are AI-related security events (e.g. abnormal traffic, errors) visible to the SOC?

AU-DGP-4.1.original: Will any PII or regulated data be used for training/fine-tuning or vendor inference?
AU-DGP-4.1.rephrase_or_note: Will personal or regulated data be fed into the AI (for training, or sent to an AI vendor)?

AU-DGP-4.2.original: Do data residency or cross-border transfers apply (cloud region, vendor location)?

AU-DGP-4.3.original: Are PII redaction / anonymisation pipelines applied before model input where feasible?

AU-AIG-4.1.original: Which AI components (GenAI, IDP, classifiers, recommenders, agents) influence decisions in this automation?
AU-AIG-4.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: from the functional description / classification.

AU-AIG-4.2.original: Will model performance be monitored across relevant segments (language, demography, document type)?

AU-AIG-4.3.original: Is there a confidence threshold with human review for low confidence? Are overrides logged?
AU-AIG-4.3.rephrase_or_note: When the AI isn't confident, should those cases go to a person to decide?

AU-AIG-4.4.original: Will each model have a short model card (purpose, data, metrics, risks, update cadence)?

AU-INF-5.1.original: Are bot, orchestrator, and AI identities integrated into enterprise zero-trust (device/user/workload identity, segmentation)?

AU-INF-5.2.original: Are automation/AI activities monitored with tailored anomaly rules in the SIEM?

AU-INF-5.3.original: Do we run purple-team or tabletop exercises focused on automation/AI-related incidents (data leaks, rogue bots)?

AU-DGP-5.1.original: Are all automations registered with data assets, tags, owners, and lineage in a catalogue?

AU-DGP-5.2.original: Is there automated reporting against data policies (retention, residency, access) across platforms?

AU-AIG-5.1.original: Will a central AI/Automation Ethics Board review high-impact use cases?
AU-AIG-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: triggered when impact level = high (flag for Ethics Board).

AU-AIG-5.2.original: Are employees informed about task/process mining and monitoring scope?
AU-AIG-5.2.rephrase_or_note: Will employees be told their tasks/processes are being monitored or mined for this?

AU-AIG-5.3.original: Is there a central risk registry with periodic audits, KPIs/KRIs (drift, bias, incident rates)?

GA-INF-1.1.original: How are model API keys and access to the GenAI service protected (RBAC, MFA, network restrictions)?

GA-INF-1.2.original: Could any prompts or outputs expose secrets or sensitive operational details, even if we don't intend to send such data?
GA-INF-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Prompt/output leakage review. Informed by Seg 4 sensitivity.

GA-INF-1.3.original: Are we restricting use to approved providers only (no shadow AI / unapproved SaaS)?

GA-DGP-1.1.original: Could any prompts or outputs contain personal or Restricted data? If yes, what is the lawful basis and do we need a DPIA for this third-party model?
GA-DGP-1.1.rephrase_or_note: Could what people type in, or the answers it gives, include personal or confidential data? (Lawful basis/DPIA tags to Dev/Sec/DPO.)

GA-DGP-1.2.original: Do we have a DPA with the model provider, and do we know how long they retain and use our prompts/outputs?

GA-AIG-1.1.original: What is the impact of this use case on people and the business (is it clearly low-impact and assistive only)?
GA-AIG-1.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: impact level from Seg 2 + suggest-vs-act from Seg 3.

GA-AIG-1.2.original: Are we enforcing prohibited content and tone-of-voice guardrails appropriate to our brand and policies?
GA-AIG-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Are there topics, language, or tones it must avoid to fit your brand and policies?

GA-AIG-1.3.original: Do we need any minimal explainability for users (Tier 1: simple 'how this was generated' explanation)?
GA-AIG-1.3.rephrase_or_note: Do users need a simple note on how the answer was produced?

GA-INF-2.1.original: Who can create, edit, or approve exemplar prompts, and is there an audit trail for those changes?

GA-INF-2.2.original: Have we checked that exemplars cannot accidentally leak credentials, internal configs, or sensitive system behaviour?

GA-INF-2.3.original: Have we run basic adversarial/prompt-injection tests against our exemplar-based prompts before wider rollout?

GA-DGP-2.1.original: Do exemplar prompts contain any personal data, customer data, or production data, and is that justified and documented?
GA-DGP-2.1.rephrase_or_note: Will the example prompts that guide it use any real customer or production data?

GA-DGP-2.2.original: Is provenance, consent, and classification for exemplar data recorded (source, permitted use, retention)?

GA-DGP-2.3.original: Does our agreement with the provider clearly state whether exemplars/prompts can or cannot be used for their own training?

GA-AIG-2.1.original: Could the choice of exemplars introduce systematic bias or unfair treatment (e.g. only one type of case, region, or persona)?
GA-AIG-2.1.rephrase_or_note: Could the examples it learns from be one-sided and lead to unfair or skewed results?

GA-AIG-2.2.original: Does the use case remain low/medium impact, or do we now require HITL before acting on outputs?

GA-AIG-2.3.original: Are prompt/exemplar changes reviewed and approved by someone accountable (not just edited ad-hoc)?

GA-INF-3.1.original: Who has write access to the RAG corpus and pipelines, and how is that access technically enforced (roles, service accounts, CI/CD)?

GA-INF-3.2.original: Are the vector store, embedding service, and RAG APIs isolated and monitored (network segmentation, logging, SOC integration)?

GA-INF-3.3.original: What controls detect and respond to RAG corpus poisoning (malicious or manipulated documents entering the KB)?

GA-DGP-3.1.original: For each RAG corpus, who is the data owner, what is the highest classification, and what lawful basis covers its use?
GA-DGP-3.1.rephrase_or_note: For the documents/knowledge it draws on — who owns that content and how sensitive is it? (Lawful basis tags to Dev/Sec/DPO.)

GA-DGP-3.2.original: Are PII and Restricted data in the corpus subject to documented ingestion rules (redaction, minimisation, retention, deletion)?

GA-DGP-3.3.original: For third-party content sources (feeds/APIs), do we have licence, IP rights, and data-protection terms defined and recorded?
GA-DGP-3.3.rephrase_or_note: Does it rely on any third-party or licensed content/feeds? (Licence/IP terms tag to Legal.)

GA-AIG-3.1.original: Do we need Tier 2 explainability (showing sources/citations and timestamps) for this use case to be trustworthy?
GA-AIG-3.1.rephrase_or_note: Should it show the sources/citations behind its answers?

GA-AIG-3.2.original: Have we considered fairness and representativeness of the RAG corpus (does it skew towards certain groups, geographies, or viewpoints)?
GA-AIG-3.2.rephrase_or_note: Could the knowledge it draws on be skewed toward certain groups, regions, or viewpoints?

GA-AIG-3.3.original: Are authority boundaries clearly defined (read-only vs. recommend vs. act), and who is accountable for the KB's ethical impact?

GA-INF-4.1.original: Are training and inference environments for adapters/LoRA weights isolated and hardened (network, IAM, secrets management)?

GA-INF-4.2.original: How are model artefacts (adapters, configs, checkpoints) signed, versioned, and validated before deployment?

GA-INF-4.3.original: Have we run adversarial and abuse-case tests specifically on the tuned model (not just the base one)?

GA-DGP-4.1.original: Is there a complete lineage and licensing view for all datasets used for fine-tuning (including third-party or user-generated data)?
GA-DGP-4.1.rephrase_or_note: What data would be used to customise/train the model for your domain? (Full lineage/licensing tags to Dev/Sec/Legal.)

GA-DGP-4.2.original: If personal data is used in training, how will we handle data-subject rights requests (access, rectification, erasure) for trained models?

GA-DGP-4.3.original: Do vendor contracts and internal policies clearly state that fine-tuning data remains our asset and cannot be repurposed without approval?

GA-AIG-4.1.original: For this tuned model, what fairness and performance metrics must we track across which key groups or segments?
GA-AIG-4.1.rephrase_or_note: Are there particular groups or segments where it's important the AI performs fairly and accurately?

GA-AIG-4.2.original: Does this use case require dual approval (Business Owner + Model Owner) and HITL for High-Impact decisions?

GA-AIG-4.3.original: Will we re-run bias, robustness, and explainability checks after each re-tuning cycle, and who signs off the results?

GA-INF-5.1.original: Do we have a complete SBOM and security assessment for all model, data, and ML framework dependencies involved in training?

GA-INF-5.2.original: How do we detect and mitigate model-level attacks (membership inference, model inversion, sophisticated jailbreaks)?

GA-INF-5.3.original: Are distributed training and serving clusters architected to prevent lateral movement and protect full model weights?

GA-DGP-5.1.original: Has a DPIA and formal data-protection/governance review been completed for the full training corpus, including regionality and cross-border transfers?
GA-DGP-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Full-corpus DPIA. Trigger: Seg 4 sensitivity = yes.

GA-DGP-5.2.original: Are records of processing (e.g. GDPR Art. 30) maintained for this model and its training data, including categories of data subjects and purposes?

GA-DGP-5.3.original: Do DPAs and contracts guarantee deletion, export, and migration rights for training data, embeddings, and logs if we change provider or exit?

GA-AIG-5.1.original: Does this model fall into a High-Risk category (e.g. under the EU AI Act or internal policy), and if so, have we mapped our obligations?
GA-AIG-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: high-risk flag from impact + domain. Formal mapping tags to Governance.

GA-AIG-5.2.original: What ongoing post-market monitoring and incident-response processes are in place for harms, bias shifts, or systemic errors, and who owns them?

GA-AIG-5.3.original: Do we require external or independent assurance (regulator, external auditor, ethics board) before and after deployment given the model's impact?
GA-AIG-5.3.rephrase_or_note: Auto: flag from impact/failure-impact. Formal decision tags to Governance.

AA-INF-1.1.original: Do all tools the agent can call use least-privilege credentials and secure storage (vault, scoped tokens)?

AA-INF-1.2.original: Are all tool calls (including parameters and results metadata) logged and available to the SOC?

AA-INF-1.3.original: Is the agent strictly prevented from calling arbitrary endpoints or tools not on an approved list?

AA-DGP-1.1.original: What data does each tool expose to or receive from the agent, and what is the highest classification?
AA-DGP-1.1.rephrase_or_note: For each system it connects to, what kind of data does that system hold, and how sensitive is it?

AA-DGP-1.2.original: Are any external APIs receiving personal or Restricted data, and do we have appropriate DPAs and lawful basis?
AA-DGP-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Will it send any data to outside/third-party services? (DPA/lawful basis tags to Dev/Sec/Legal.)

AA-DGP-1.3.original: How long are tool-call logs (containing any PII) retained, and under what governance?

AA-AIG-1.1.original: Is this agent strictly assistive (no direct writes or material decisions), and is that enforced technically?

AA-AIG-1.2.original: Do users understand when the agent has called tools and what those tools did (basic transparency)?
AA-AIG-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Should users be able to see what actions/tools the agent used to get its result?

AA-AIG-1.3.original: Is the impact of this use case on people and the business clearly low-risk?

AA-INF-2.1.original: Can the planner chain tools only within approved patterns, or could it combine them in unexpected, risky ways?

AA-INF-2.2.original: Is there a hard limit on the number of tool calls / steps per request to prevent runaway behaviour?

AA-INF-2.3.original: Have we tested prompt-injection scenarios where a user tries to coerce the agent into using tools dangerously?

AA-DGP-2.1.original: Does any configuration or example data used to set up the agent contain real personal or production data?
AA-DGP-2.1.rephrase_or_note: Will any real personal or production data be used to set up or configure the agent?

AA-DGP-2.2.original: Can the planner cause personal data from one system to be copied into another where it wasn't previously present?
AA-DGP-2.2.rephrase_or_note: Cross-system data movement. Trigger: Seg 4 sensitivity = yes.

AA-DGP-2.3.original: Are cross-system data flows described and approved in a simple data-flow diagram for this agent?

AA-AIG-2.1.original: For which actions is the agent allowed to execute changes automatically, and which must remain HITL?

AA-AIG-2.2.original: Are the agent's plans visible and editable before it carries out a sequence of actions?
AA-AIG-2.2.rephrase_or_note: Before it carries out a sequence of steps, should a person be able to see and adjust the plan?

AA-AIG-2.3.original: Who approves new tools or actions being added to this agent's capabilities?

AA-INF-3.1.original: For each tool, what system(s) can it modify, and are those exposed actions strictly scoped and monitored?

AA-INF-3.2.original: Are RAG/KB and transactional tools separated (reading knowledge vs. changing records) at the IAM and network level?

AA-INF-3.3.original: Do we have alerts for anomalous tool sequences (mass updates, repeated high-risk actions)?

AA-DGP-3.1.original: Which RAG/KB sources can the agent query, and who owns and governs those datasets?
AA-DGP-3.1.rephrase_or_note: Which knowledge sources or databases should it be allowed to look at, and who owns them?

AA-DGP-3.2.original: Does the agent ever pass full records or large payloads between tools where a reduced view would suffice?

AA-DGP-3.3.original: For third-party tools or SaaS, do we know whether they store or reuse the data the agent sends?
AA-DGP-3.3.rephrase_or_note: Third-party data reuse terms. Trigger: Seg 5 external service = yes.

AA-AIG-3.1.original: For each workflow, which steps are fully automated vs. subject to HITL, and is this documented in an agent card or runbook?
AA-AIG-3.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: HITL boundary (Seg 6). Agent card/runbook tags to Dev.

AA-AIG-3.2.original: Are there any user groups or cases where automatic actions could systematically disadvantage or harm them?
AA-AIG-3.2.rephrase_or_note: Are there any groups who could be systematically disadvantaged by the actions it takes automatically?

AA-AIG-3.3.original: Are we regularly reviewing agent logs for harmful, inappropriate, or unexpected behaviours and correcting them?

AA-INF-4.1.original: Does each agent role have its own identity and permissions, and can we see which agent performed which action?

AA-INF-4.2.original: Can we cleanly stop or pause a specific workflow or agent if it behaves unexpectedly?

AA-INF-4.3.original: Are inter-agent communications protected against spoofing or injection (identity/auth on messages)?

AA-DGP-4.1.original: Do different agents in the system have clearly separated data scopes (e.g. HR agent cannot see finance data)?

AA-DGP-4.2.original: Are cross-domain agent workflows reviewed for privacy, purpose limitation, and legal basis?
AA-DGP-4.2.rephrase_or_note: Does it combine data or actions across different business domains (e.g. HR and Finance)? (Privacy review tags to Dev/Sec.)

AA-DGP-4.3.original: Can we trace which agent accessed or changed a given person's data, and when?

AA-AIG-4.1.original: Does each agent in the system have a clearly documented role, scope, and authority boundary, and who owns it?
AA-AIG-4.1.rephrase_or_note: Per-agent role/authority docs. Owner from Seg 1.

AA-AIG-4.2.original: Can any single agent bypass oversight, or does the coordinator enforce global constraints (dual-control, HITL)?

AA-AIG-4.3.original: Are we evaluating emergent behaviours of the system (not just single agents) for fairness, stability, and misuse?

AA-INF-5.1.original: Are long-lived agents constrained by time, scope, and budgets, with explicit 'no-go' zones (systems they cannot touch)?
AA-INF-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: no-go zones from Seg 6 'must never do'. Time/budget caps tag to Dev.

AA-INF-5.2.original: Do we continuously test for emergent security issues (e.g. agents teaching each other unsafe patterns)?

AA-INF-5.3.original: Is there a tested emergency shutdown process that reliably halts all autonomous agent activity without breaking critical operations?

AA-DGP-5.1.original: For autonomous agents operating at scale, do we have a DPIA and a documented list of all datasets/systems they touch?

AA-DGP-5.2.original: Are agent activities included in records of processing (or equivalent) with data categories and purposes clearly described?

AA-DGP-5.3.original: If we change provider or switch off an agentic platform, can we export and delete logs, memories, and configuration containing personal data?

AA-AIG-5.1.original: Does this agentic system fall under any 'High-Risk' definition (e.g. EU AI Act), and have we mapped concrete obligations?
AA-AIG-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: high-risk flag from impact + autonomy. Formal mapping tags to Governance.

AA-AIG-5.2.original: What is our ongoing monitoring plan for harms, bias shifts, and systemic errors, and who is accountable for acting on signals?

AA-AIG-5.3.original: Do we need external/independent oversight (regulator, external auditor, ethics board) before or during operation, given the impact?
AA-AIG-5.3.rephrase_or_note: Auto: flag from impact. Formal decision tags to Governance.

DS-INF-1.1.original: Who can access sensitive dashboards/reports, and is access role-based and logged?

DS-INF-1.2.original: Can BI tools or users accidentally change production data, or are they strictly read-only?

DS-INF-1.3.original: Are ETL/BI configuration changes (e.g. KPI definitions) tracked and auditable?

DS-DGP-1.1.original: What is the highest data classification present in BI dashboards/reports, and who owns that data?
DS-DGP-1.1.rephrase_or_note: What's the most sensitive data shown in the reports, and who owns it?

DS-DGP-1.2.original: Do dashboards with personal or sensitive data enforce minimisation and role-based access?

DS-DGP-1.3.original: Are data definitions (KPIs, metrics) documented and governed?
DS-DGP-1.3.rephrase_or_note: What are the key metrics/KPIs it should report, and are their definitions agreed?

DS-AIG-1.1.original: Are we using these reports/dashboards only as decision-support, or are they effectively acting as rules without oversight?
DS-AIG-1.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: decision-support vs automated-rule from Seg 3.

DS-AIG-1.2.original: Do decision-makers understand metric definitions, data limitations, and possible blind spots?
DS-AIG-1.2.rephrase_or_note: Will the people using these reports understand the metric definitions and data limitations?

DS-AIG-1.3.original: Could the way we present metrics systematically disadvantage or overlook certain groups or regions?
DS-AIG-1.3.rephrase_or_note: Could the way the metrics are presented overlook or disadvantage certain groups or regions?

DS-INF-2.1.original: How is code and configuration (e.g. analysis scripts) version-controlled and reviewed?

DS-INF-2.2.original: Can analysts export large volumes of sensitive data without oversight?

DS-INF-2.3.original: Are there controls on data egress from analytic sandboxes (no uncontrolled local copies)?

DS-DGP-2.1.original: Are analytic sandboxes using real personal data, and if so, under what lawful basis and with what minimisation safeguards?
DS-DGP-2.1.rephrase_or_note: Will the analysis work use real personal data? (Lawful basis/safeguards tag to Dev/Sec/DPO.)

DS-DGP-2.2.original: How long do we retain analytic extracts, and where are they stored?

DS-DGP-2.3.original: Are external/purchased datasets documented with licence terms and permissible uses?
DS-DGP-2.3.rephrase_or_note: Will it use any purchased or external datasets? (Licence terms tag to Legal.)

DS-AIG-2.1.original: Are we careful not to treat correlations found in diagnostic analysis as causal without additional evidence?

DS-AIG-2.2.original: Could these analyses drive policy or operational changes that materially impact certain groups, and have we considered fairness?
DS-AIG-2.2.rephrase_or_note: Could these findings drive changes that materially affect certain groups, and has fairness been considered?

DS-AIG-2.3.original: Are key assumptions and limitations of diagnostic studies documented and communicated?

DS-INF-3.1.original: Are model APIs/score services protected with authentication, authorisation, and rate limits?

DS-INF-3.2.original: Are model-serving environments segregated (network, IAM) from user-facing applications and the open internet?

DS-INF-3.3.original: Are model calls and errors logged in a way that supports security monitoring and incident investigation?

DS-DGP-3.1.original: For each predictive model, what datasets were used (training/validation/test), and what is the lawful basis for using personal data?
DS-DGP-3.1.rephrase_or_note: What data would be used to build the prediction model? (Lawful basis tags to Dev/Sec/DPO.)

DS-DGP-3.2.original: Has a DPIA been completed for models that materially impact individuals (risk, eligibility, pricing)?
DS-DGP-3.2.rephrase_or_note: DPIA for high-impact models. Trigger: Seg 2 impact = high.

DS-DGP-3.3.original: Are retention and deletion rules defined for model training datasets and feature stores?

DS-AIG-3.1.original: What is the impact level of each predictive model on individuals and business outcomes (low/medium/high), and is this documented?

DS-AIG-3.2.original: Do we have suitable explainability mechanisms (scorecards, feature importance, local explanations) appropriate to its impact level?
DS-AIG-3.2.rephrase_or_note: How important is it that people can understand why the model predicted what it did?

DS-AIG-3.3.original: Are we tracking fairness metrics or performance by segment for models that materially affect individuals (eligibility, pricing, risk)?
DS-AIG-3.3.rephrase_or_note: Are there groups where it's important to check the model performs fairly (e.g. eligibility, pricing, risk)?

DS-INF-4.1.original: Who can change decision rules, optimisation objectives, or constraints, and how is that change governed and logged?

DS-INF-4.2.original: Are there controls to detect and stop anomalous decisions (mass denials, extreme pricing)?

DS-INF-4.3.original: Are decision engines tested and validated in pre-prod with realistic data before being allowed to influence production?

DS-DGP-4.1.original: What additional data (features, attributes) are used for prescriptive decisions, and could they introduce fairness/ethics concerns?
DS-DGP-4.1.rephrase_or_note: What extra information would feed its recommendations, and could any of it raise fairness concerns?

DS-DGP-4.2.original: Are external data sources used for decisioning governed by DPAs and contract terms covering analytic and automated use?

DS-DGP-4.3.original: Are we separating data used for optimisation from data used for explanations in a way that remains transparent and compliant?

DS-AIG-4.1.original: For prescriptive/optimisation use cases, which decisions are fully automated vs. require HITL or override capability?

DS-AIG-4.2.original: Have we done a fairness/impact assessment on how optimised decisions affect different groups of people, customers, or regions?
DS-AIG-4.2.rephrase_or_note: Should we assess how its recommended actions affect different groups, customers, or regions?

DS-AIG-4.3.original: Are decision policies and how they use model outputs documented and reviewed periodically?

DS-INF-5.1.original: Can online learning or RL agents update policies directly in prod, and if so, is there a controlled deployment pipeline with rollback?

DS-INF-5.2.original: Are experiments and policy changes in critical domains (credit, safety) subject to additional security or operational approvals?

DS-INF-5.3.original: Do we have security monitoring specifically tuned to detect harmful or anomalous patterns linked to model updates?

DS-DGP-5.1.original: Do we have a complete view of all data (including feedback logs) used by adaptive/online systems, recorded in records of processing?

DS-DGP-5.2.original: How will we handle data-subject rights (access, correction, erasure) for data that has influenced continually learning models?

DS-DGP-5.3.original: If we stop using a vendor/platform, can we export and delete training data, feedback logs, and model artefacts containing personal data?

DS-AIG-5.1.original: Do these adaptive/online systems fall into any 'High-Risk' categories (EU AI Act or internal policy), and have we mapped obligations?
DS-AIG-5.1.rephrase_or_note: Auto: high-risk flag from impact. Formal mapping tags to Governance.

DS-AIG-5.2.original: What is our plan for monitoring harms, fairness drift, and systemic errors, and who is accountable for acting on findings?

DS-AIG-5.3.original: Do we require external or independent review (internal audit, regulator, ethics board) given the scale and impact of automated decisioning?
DS-AIG-5.3.rephrase_or_note: Auto: flag from impact. Formal decision tags to Governance.

AU-L1.name: Robotic Desktop Automation (RDA)
AU-L1.decision_gate: AU-L1-TA-Gate: Approve low-impact assistive use on managed desktops; escalate to L2 if PII/critical txns.
AU-L1.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score primary; Data criteria as inputs grow; Model Complexity from L4+.

AU-L2.name: Robotic Process Automation (RPA)
AU-L2.decision_gate: AU-L2-TA-Gate: Approve stable rule-based bots w/ least-privilege+vault; escalate to L3 if multi-system/customer-facing.
AU-L2.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score primary; Data criteria as inputs grow; Model Complexity from L4+.

AU-L3.name: Orchestrated RPA
AU-L3.decision_gate: AU-L3-TA-Gate: Require orchestrator controls/HA-DR/SoD; escalate governance if high-impact or cross-BU/vendor.
AU-L3.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score primary; Data criteria as inputs grow; Model Complexity from L4+.

AU-L4.name: Intelligent Process Automation (IPA)
AU-L4.decision_gate: AU-L4-TA-Gate: Require model cards/eval/bias/HITL/DPIA; reject or redesign to L3 if AI governance unmet.
AU-L4.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score primary; Data criteria as inputs grow; Model Complexity from L4+.

AU-L5.name: Hyper-Automation
AU-L5.decision_gate: AU-L5-TA-Gate: Proceed only with mature CoE/risk registry/ethics board; else downgrade to L3/L4.
AU-L5.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score primary; Data criteria as inputs grow; Model Complexity from L4+.

GA-L1.name: Zero-Shot Prompting
GA-L1.decision_gate: GA-L1-TA-Gate: Approve low-impact non-sensitive internal use; escalate to L2+ as impact/sensitivity rises.
GA-L1.feasibility_focus: Data Availability/Quality/Maturity rise sharply from L3 (RAG) onward; Model Complexity tracks level; Tech Deployment throughout.

GA-L2.name: Few-Shot / In-Context Learning
GA-L2.decision_gate: GA-L2-TA-Gate: Approve with exemplar governance+logging; HITL/controls if beyond internal productivity.
GA-L2.feasibility_focus: Data Availability/Quality/Maturity rise sharply from L3 (RAG) onward; Model Complexity tracks level; Tech Deployment throughout.

GA-L3.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
GA-L3.decision_gate: GA-L3-TA-Gate: Require RAG controls (corpus owners, poisoning defences, DPIA, red-teaming); board review if high-impact.
GA-L3.feasibility_focus: Data Availability/Quality/Maturity rise sharply from L3 (RAG) onward; Model Complexity tracks level; Tech Deployment throughout.

GA-L4.name: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT)
GA-L4.decision_gate: GA-L4-TA-Gate: Require PEFT eval/bias/dual approval; HITL for high-impact; reject/redesign if governance unmet.
GA-L4.feasibility_focus: Data Availability/Quality/Maturity rise sharply from L3 (RAG) onward; Model Complexity tracks level; Tech Deployment throughout.

GA-L5.name: Full Fine-Tuning / Continual Pre-training
GA-L5.decision_gate: GA-L5-TA-Gate: Proceed only with full ISO 42001-style governance + AI Act obligations; else downgrade to L4/L3.
GA-L5.feasibility_focus: Data Availability/Quality/Maturity rise sharply from L3 (RAG) onward; Model Complexity tracks level; Tech Deployment throughout.

AA-L1.name: Tool-Calling Agent
AA-L1.decision_gate: AA-L1-TA-Gate: Approve assistive read-only use; escalate if write permissions or sensitive data requested.
AA-L1.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score dominate (tools/systems); Data criteria per connected source; Model Complexity from level.

AA-L2.name: Single-Agent Planner
AA-L2.decision_gate: AA-L2-TA-Gate: Approve if HITL boundaries clear+tested; escalate to L3+ if cross-system/sensitive domains.
AA-L2.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score dominate (tools/systems); Data criteria per connected source; Model Complexity from level.

AA-L3.name: Orchestrating Agent (Multi-Tool)
AA-L3.decision_gate: AA-L3-TA-Gate: Require robust InfoSec/Data/AI controls (tool scoping, HITL, agent cards, DPAs); govern high-impact.
AA-L3.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score dominate (tools/systems); Data criteria per connected source; Model Complexity from level.

AA-L4.name: Multi-Agent System
AA-L4.decision_gate: AA-L4-TA-Gate: Proceed only if roles/authority/oversight defined; require multi-agent governance reviews+monitoring.
AA-L4.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score dominate (tools/systems); Data criteria per connected source; Model Complexity from level.

AA-L5.name: Autonomous Enterprise Agent
AA-L5.decision_gate: AA-L5-TA-Gate: Proceed only with enterprise lifecycle governance/monitoring; else downgrade to L3/L4 w/ stricter HITL.
AA-L5.feasibility_focus: Tech Deployment + Variables/Process score dominate (tools/systems); Data criteria per connected source; Model Complexity from level.

DS-L1.name: Descriptive Analytics / BI
DS-L1.decision_gate: DS-L1-TA-Gate: Approve descriptive reporting w/ standard security+data governance; escalate if used as rules.
DS-L1.feasibility_focus: All Data criteria central; UI/UX criterion at L1 (dashboards); Model Complexity rises L3->L5; Tech Deployment throughout.

DS-L2.name: Diagnostic Analytics
DS-L2.decision_gate: DS-L2-TA-Gate: Approve w/ documented assumptions/limits; escalate to L3 if findings drive major policy change.
DS-L2.feasibility_focus: All Data criteria central; UI/UX criterion at L1 (dashboards); Model Complexity rises L3->L5; Tech Deployment throughout.

DS-L3.name: Predictive Modelling
DS-L3.decision_gate: DS-L3-TA-Gate: Require model docs/explainability/fairness/monitoring + Business+Model Owner approvals.
DS-L3.feasibility_focus: All Data criteria central; UI/UX criterion at L1 (dashboards); Model Complexity rises L3->L5; Tech Deployment throughout.

DS-L4.name: Prescriptive Analytics & Optimisation
DS-L4.decision_gate: DS-L4-TA-Gate: Require HITL/override + mandatory fairness/impact assessment; board approval for critical use.
DS-L4.feasibility_focus: All Data criteria central; UI/UX criterion at L1 (dashboards); Model Complexity rises L3->L5; Tech Deployment throughout.

DS-L5.name: Adaptive / Online Decisioning
DS-L5.decision_gate: DS-L5-TA-Gate: Proceed only w/ full lifecycle governance/monitoring; else redesign to L3/L4 w/ slower updates.
DS-L5.feasibility_focus: All Data criteria central; UI/UX criterion at L1 (dashboards); Model Complexity rises L3->L5; Tech Deployment throughout.

AU-L1.name: Robotic Desktop Automation
AU-L1.default_verdict: APPROVE - assistive use on managed desktop, minimal controls
AU-L1.escalate_up: Sensitive/PII data, critical transactions, or persistent local sensitive storage -> AU-L2

AU-L2.name: Robotic Process Automation
AU-L2.default_verdict: APPROVE - stable rule-based bot, least-privilege + vault + logging + SOPs
AU-L2.escalate_up: Spans multiple critical systems, BUs, or customer-facing journeys -> AU-L3

AU-L3.name: Orchestrated RPA
AU-L3.default_verdict: APPROVE - full orchestrator controls, HA/DR, SoD, audit, basic data governance
AU-L3.overlay: High impact (key journeys, payments, regulatory) OR cross-BU/vendor sharing -> impact assessment, DPAs/DPIA, fairness checks on routing, CoE/board review

AU-L4.name: Intelligent Process Automation
AU-L4.default_verdict: APPROVE - model cards, eval+bias, confidence thresholds + HITL, retrain cadence, DPIA
AU-L4.overlay: High-stakes (eligibility, complaints, HR, finance) -> DPIA, model monitoring, HITL, ethics review
AU-L4.redesign_or_reject: AI drives high-stakes/regulated decisions AND governance (DPIA, monitoring, HITL, ethics) cannot be met -> REJECT or redesign to AU-L3 rule-based

AU-L5.name: Hyper-Automation
AU-L5.default_verdict: Proceed only with mature CoE, portfolio risk registry, harmonised data governance, zero-trust, Ethics Board
AU-L5.overlay: Always portfolio-level (ethics board, risk registry, workforce impact)
AU-L5.redesign_or_reject: Portfolio oversight/governance weak (no central view, fragmented standards) -> downgrade to AU-L3/L4; uplift governance first

GA-L1.name: Zero-Shot Prompting
GA-L1.default_verdict: APPROVE - low-impact, non-sensitive internal use, minimal controls
GA-L1.escalate_up: Impact or data sensitivity increases -> GA-L2+

GA-L2.name: Few-Shot / In-Context
GA-L2.escalate_up: Used beyond internal productivity -> require HITL / additional controls (toward GA-L3)

GA-L3.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
GA-L3.default_verdict: APPROVE - full RAG controls: corpus owners, poisoning defences, Tier-2 explainability, red-teaming, hardening
GA-L3.overlay: High impact -> board-level governance.  Personal data in corpus -> DPIA

GA-L4.name: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
GA-L4.default_verdict: APPROVE - PEFT eval, bias testing, dual approvals, HITL for high-impact
GA-L4.overlay: High-impact decisions -> dual owner approval + HITL + bias regression
GA-L4.redesign_or_reject: Governance requirements cannot be met -> REJECT or redesign to GA-L3

GA-L5.name: Full Fine-Tuning
GA-L5.default_verdict: Proceed only with full ISO 42001-style governance + AI Act obligations + clear business justification
GA-L5.overlay: Always: Ethics Board approval, post-market monitoring, independent assurance for high-risk
GA-L5.redesign_or_reject: Full governance / AI Act obligations cannot be met -> downgrade to GA-L4/L3

AA-L1.name: Tool-Calling Agent
AA-L1.default_verdict: APPROVE - assistive only, read + draft, writes prohibited
AA-L1.escalate_up: Write permissions OR sensitive data requested -> AA-L2+

AA-L2.name: Single-Agent Planner
AA-L2.default_verdict: APPROVE - clear + enforced HITL boundaries, planner tested
AA-L2.escalate_up: Cross-system actions OR sensitive domains -> AA-L3+ controls

AA-L3.name: Orchestrating Agent
AA-L3.default_verdict: APPROVE - tool scoping, RAG governance, HITL, logs, agent cards, DPAs

AA-L4.name: Multi-Agent System
AA-L4.default_verdict: Proceed only if roles, authority, oversight clearly defined; multi-agent governance reviews + strong monitoring
AA-L4.overlay: Always: per-agent ownership, coordinator constraints, emergent-behaviour review
AA-L4.redesign_or_reject: Roles/authority/oversight not clearly definable -> do not proceed at L4; redesign to AA-L3

AA-L5.name: Autonomous Enterprise Agent
AA-L5.default_verdict: Proceed only with full lifecycle governance, monitoring, regulatory alignment
AA-L5.overlay: Always: EU AI Act high-risk assessment, ethics board, post-market monitoring
AA-L5.redesign_or_reject: Enterprise lifecycle governance/monitoring cannot be supported -> downgrade to AA-L3/L4 with stricter HITL

DS-L1.name: Descriptive / BI
DS-L1.default_verdict: APPROVE - descriptive reporting, standard security + data governance
DS-L1.escalate_up: Used for thresholds/rules without explicit governance -> escalate (toward DS-L3 governance)

DS-L2.name: Diagnostic Analytics
DS-L2.escalate_up: Insights will drive major policy/operational change -> DS-L3-level governance (model risk + fairness)

DS-L3.name: Predictive Modelling
DS-L3.default_verdict: APPROVE - model docs, explainability, fairness checks, monitoring + Business Owner + Model Owner approvals
DS-L3.overlay: Materially affects individuals (eligibility, pricing, risk) -> fairness metrics + explainability + DPIA

DS-L4.name: Prescriptive & Optimisation
DS-L4.overlay: Critical use cases -> Governance Board / senior committee approval

DS-L5.name: Adaptive / Online Decisioning
DS-L5.default_verdict: Proceed only with full lifecycle governance + monitoring + regulatory obligations
DS-L5.overlay: Always: high-risk assessment, harms/drift monitoring, independent review
DS-L5.redesign_or_reject: Full lifecycle governance/monitoring cannot be supported -> redesign to DS-L3/L4 with slower update cycles + stronger human control
